skill.name: reasoningbank-with-agentdb
skill.description: Implement ReasoningBank adaptive learning patterns with AgentDB. The skill explains trajectory tracking, verdict judgment, memory distillation, pattern retrieval, migration, and MCP integration for learning agents.
skill.summary: Build adaptive ReasoningBank memory workflows with AgentDB-backed retrieval.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected many shell command examples, local filesystem paths, and outbound documentation links, but the file is a documentation-only skill. No malicious intent or prompt injection was found; publication is reasonable with a warning about running remote npx commands and MCP setup.

medium_findings.title: Version-Unpinned Remote CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest and to register an AgentDB MCP server. This is expected setup behavior, but it executes a remotely resolved package and can expand local tool access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and semantically intended to execute remote npm package code and install an MCP integration. The context is legitimate setup documentation, so this is a medium operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local Hidden Database Access
medium_findings.description: The examples create, read, migrate, and export SQLite databases under hidden directories such as .agentdb and .swarm. This can touch agent memory data and should be reviewed before use in sensitive workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database paths and migration commands are clearly present and target hidden local directories. They appear to support normal memory storage and migration, with no evidence of credential theft or remote exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Informational External Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the AgentDB GitHub repository and project website. They are documentation links and do not perform network access by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Learn More references, not executable code. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography static alerts appear to match unrelated descriptive text in the skill metadata and headings. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about ReasoningBank and AgentDB modules, not cryptographic implementation. This makes the blocker-class weak crypto findings likely heuristic false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many fenced CLI examples that run npx, claude mcp, ls, import, export, migrate, and stats commands. Users should inspect package provenance and paths before executing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and numerous. They are examples in Markdown rather than code that executes automatically.

dangerous_patterns.title: MCP Server Registration
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start directs users to add an AgentDB MCP server through Claude Code. MCP tools can extend agent capabilities, so users should only register trusted packages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MCP setup commands are directly cited in the documentation. No malicious MCP behavior is shown in this skill file, but tool registration is a meaningful trust boundary.

content.value_statement: Agent teams need a durable way to learn from prior trajectories and outcomes. This skill shows how to use AgentDB with ReasoningBank patterns for retrieval, judgment, distillation, and migration.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to initialize an AgentDB-backed ReasoningBank database.
Shows patterns for storing trajectories, experiences, distilled memories, and principles.
Demonstrates retrieval with reasoning options such as MMR, context synthesis, and memory optimization.
Covers migration from legacy ReasoningBank memory databases.
Documents CLI operations for stats, import, export, and troubleshooting.
limitations: Requires Node.js and external AgentDB packages to run the examples.
Does not include executable source files or tests in the skill package.
Performance claims depend on the referenced AgentDB implementation and workload.
Users must validate local database paths before running migration or export commands.

use_cases.title: Build learning agents
use_cases.description: Create agents that retrieve similar past experiences before choosing an approach.

use_cases.title: Migrate agent memory
use_cases.description: Move legacy ReasoningBank memory into an AgentDB-backed database with validation steps.

use_cases.title: Improve decision review
use_cases.description: Use trajectory judgment and memory distillation to compare new work with successful patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Set up ReasoningBank
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a safe first setup for ReasoningBank with AgentDB in my project. Include prerequisites, commands to review, and database paths.

prompt_templates.title: Design trajectory storage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a trajectory tracking schema for an agent that solves support tickets. Include fields for steps, verdicts, metrics, and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate existing memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a migration plan from a legacy ReasoningBank SQLite database to AgentDB. Include validation and rollback checks.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize adaptive retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to tune retrieval, MMR diversity, context synthesis, and memory optimization for a multi-domain agent memory system.

output_examples.input: Plan ReasoningBank setup for a new code review agent.
output: A setup checklist with Node.js, AgentDB, and MCP review steps.
A proposed local database path and embedding dimension.
A warning to pin package versions before production use.

output_examples.input: Design memory distillation for API optimization tasks.
output: A trajectory format for task steps, outcomes, and latency metrics.
A retrieval plan for similar successful optimizations.
A distillation rule that promotes repeated successful fixes into reusable patterns.

output_examples.input: Review a legacy memory migration plan.
output: A migration sequence covering backup, conversion, stats checks, and validation.
A list of sensitive local paths to verify before execution.
A publication warning about remote CLI commands and MCP trust boundaries.

best_practices: Pin AgentDB package versions before using the CLI in production workspaces.
Back up legacy memory databases before running migration or optimization steps.
Keep domains, confidence scores, and outcome metrics consistent across stored experiences.
anti_patterns: Running npx commands from documentation without reviewing package provenance.
Mixing unrelated domains in one memory collection without labels or filters.
Treating similarity scores as final judgments without checking task context.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide with CLI and TypeScript examples for AgentDB and ReasoningBank workflows.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The guide also references Claude Code MCP setup.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks users to run remote npx commands and register an MCP server. These actions are useful but require trust review.

faq.question: Can it migrate existing ReasoningBank memory?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide describes migration from a legacy SQLite memory database to an AgentDB-backed database.

faq.question: Does it send data to external URLs?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The URLs in the skill are documentation links, not automatic network requests.

faq.question: What should users verify before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should verify package versions, local database paths, backups, and MCP server trust before executing setup commands.
